revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:彦坐王]][[ko:히코이마스노미코]][[zh:彥坐王]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|spouse={{ill|Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime|qid=Q134879672|1=Okitsunagamizu-no-Irehime}}; {{ill|Q106112059|qid=Q106112059|1=Enatsu-hime no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q13360104|qid=Q13360104|1=Saho no Ōkuramito-me}}|children={{ill|Empress Sahohime|qid=Q11571754|1=Saho-hime}}; {{ill|Prince Sahohiko|qid=Q11571755|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto}}; {{ill|Q106383029|qid=Q106383029|1=Mizunoho no Mawaka no O}}; {{ill|Q11626514|qid=Q11626514|1=Ozaho no Ōkimi}}; {{ill|Q119380336|qid=Q119380336|1=Kami Ōne}}; {{ill|Tanba no Michinushi|qid=Q11368587|1=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka|qid=Q11466268|1=Yamashiro-no-Otsutsukimawaka}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|1=Emperor Kaika}}|mother={{ill|Oketsu-hime no mikoto|qid=Q97496338|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto}}|relatives={{ill|Princess Mimatsuhime|qid=Q11489233|1=Princess Mimatsuhime}}; {{ill|Sujin|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}}}}

'''Hikoimasu no Ōkimi''' (彦坐王, dates unknown) was a member of the {{ill|Japanese Imperial family|ja|皇族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Imperial family|1=Imperial Family of Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11581068}} during ancient Japan, as recorded in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}.

== Overview ==

In the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', he is referred to as "Hikoimasu no Ōkimi," while in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he is named "Hikoimasu no Mikoto" (日子坐王). Other texts also refer to him as "Hikoimasu no Mikoto" or "Hikoimasu no Ōkimi." He was the third prince of {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}}, the ninth emperor of Japan, and is noted as the great-grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. Although few records detail his life, he is significant in the genealogical narratives presented in the ''Kojiki.''

== Genealogy ==

[[File:Kaikatennou oujintennou.png|thumb|200px|Genealogical relationship of Hikoimasu no Ōkimi]]
According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', Hikoimasu no Ōkimi was born to {{ill|Emperor Kaika|en|Emperor Kaika|qid=Q333548|lt=Emperor Kaika|1=Emperor Kaika}} and his consort, {{ill|Utsuhime|ja|姥津媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 意祁都比売命|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97496338}}, the younger sister of {{ill|Hahatsu no Mikoto|ja|姥津命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hahatsu no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the progenitor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|qid=Q11417873|lt=Wani clan|1=Wani clan}}. The only child mentioned in the ''Nihon Shoki'' is {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}}, though there is conflicting tradition naming him the son of {{ill|Hiko Yasugumi no Ōkimi|ja|彦湯産隅王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiko Yasugumi no Ōkimi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' Hikoimasu is the third son of Emperor Kaika and {{ill|Oketuhime|ja|意祁都比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oketuhime|1=Oketsu-hime no mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97496338}}, a younger sister of Hahatsu no Mikoto. His offspring, including notable figures such as Tanba Michinushi and Sahohiko, are listed below:
* Consort: {{ill|Enatsu Hime|ja|山代之荏名津比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enatsu Hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, daughter of Nagamizo no Mikoto.
** {{ill|Ōmata no Mikoto|ja|大俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Komata no Mikoto|ja|小俣王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
** {{ill|Shibumi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja|志夫美宿禰王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shibumi no Sukune no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578848|1=Shibumi no Sukune no Mikoto}}
* Consort: {{ill|Saho no Ōkuramito-me|ja|沙本之大闇見戸売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saho no Ōkuramito-me|qq=}}
** {{ill|Sahohiko no Ōkimi|ja|狭穂彦王|1=Sahohiko no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Ōkimi|13=User:Immanuelle/Sahohiko no Mikoto|qq=}}
** {{ill|Ozaho no Ōkimi|ja|袁邪本王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ozaho no Ōkimi|qq=}}
** {{ill|Saho-hime|en|Saho-hime|qid=Q11571754|lt=Sahohime no Mikoto|1=Saho-hime}}
** {{ill|Murobiko no Mikoto|ja|室毘古王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murobiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578852|1=Murobiko no Mikoto}}
* Consort: {{ill|Okinaga no Mizuyori-hime no Mikoto|ja|息長水依比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga no Mizuyori-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578857|1=Okinaga no Mizuyori-hime no Mikoto}} daughter of {{ill|Ame-no-Mikage-no-Kami|ja|天之御影神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-mikage-no-mikoto|qq=}}
** {{ill|Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba-no-Michinushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|ja|丹波道主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tanba Michinushi no Mikoto|qq=}}
** {{ill|Mizuho no Mawaka no Mikoto|ja|水之穂真若王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho no Mawaka no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578859|1=Mizuho no Mawaka no Mikoto}}
** {{ill|Kamu no Ōne no Mikoto|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamu no Ōne no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}
** {{ill|Mizuho Ioyori-hime|ja|水穂五百依比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuho Ioyori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578864|1=Mizuho Ioyori-hime}}
** {{ill|Miitsu-hime|ja|御井津比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miitsu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578867|1=Miitsu-hime}}

== Records ==

The ''Nihon Shoki'' contains no detailed accounts of his life. The ''Kojiki'' narrates a tradition in which Hikoimasu was sent to {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} by Emperor Sujin to suppress rebellion, notably defeating {{ill|Kugamimi no Mikasa|ja|玖賀耳之御笠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugamimi no Mikasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578871|1=Kugamimi no Mikasa}}.

== Burial ==

The {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|qid=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates his burial site as the {{ill|Hikoimasu no Mikoto Tomb|ja|日子坐命墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikoimasu no Mikoto Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Iwata, Gifu|ja|岐阜市岩田西|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwata, Gifu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Gifu Prefecture.<ref>Imperial Household Ministry, ''Ryo Bo Yōran'' (1934).</ref> Nearby stands the {{ill|Iwano Nishi Shrine|ja|伊波乃西神社|1=Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwano Nishi Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwa-no-Nishi Shrine|qq=}}, dedicated to Hikoimasu.

== Descendants ==

Hikoimasu is credited in the ''Kojiki'' as the progenitor of numerous clans, including the {{ill|Hime-dara|ja|比売陀君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hime-dara|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tamade clan|ja|当麻勾君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamade clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sasa clan|ja|佐佐君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasa clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|長幡部連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
